hero.headline: Cyclone Yaas Aftermath: Power Restored to 90% of Affected Areas in West Bengal
hero.subheadline: Over 1.5 million people still without electricity as restoration efforts continue
hero.summary: The government of West Bengal has reported that electricity has been restored to 90% of the areas affected by Cyclone Yaas, which made landfall on May 26. Over 1.5 million people are still without power, with the most affected districts being Purba Medinipur, North 24 Parganas, and South 24 Parganas. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has announced that restoration work is ongoing and that power supply will be fully restored within the next 48 hours. The state government has also set up relief camps and distributed relief materials to those affected by the cyclone.
hero.impact: Agricultural and fishing industries face immediate losses; infrastructure damage assessed.
hero.unknowns: Full extent of crop and livestock damage still unclear

stories.headline: West Bengal Government Announces Compensation for Cyclone-Hit Farmers
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has announced a compensation package for farmers whose crops were damaged by Cyclone Yaas. The government will provide Rs 10,000 per hectare to farmers who have suffered losses. The government has also announced that it will provide seeds and fertilizers to farmers at subsidized rates to help them replant their crops. The government has estimated that the total cost of the compensation package will be around Rs 500 crore.

stories.headline: West Bengal Government Launches New Scheme to Improve Healthcare Infrastructure
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has launched a new scheme to improve healthcare infrastructure in the state. The scheme, called 'Swasthya Sathi', aims to provide free healthcare to all residents of the state. The government has announced that it will set up new hospitals and healthcare centers in rural areas and upgrade existing ones. The government has also announced that it will provide free medicine and diagnostic services to all residents of the state.

stories.headline: West Bengal Government to Provide Free Education to Students Affected by Cyclone Yaas
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has announced that it will provide free education to students who have been affected by Cyclone Yaas. The government will provide free textbooks, uniforms, and stationery to students in affected areas. The government has also announced that it will provide financial assistance to students who have lost their homes or belongings in the cyclone. The government has estimated that the total cost of the scheme will be around Rs 100 crore.

stories.headline: West Bengal Government Announces New Scheme to Provide Jobs to Migrant Workers
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has announced a new scheme to provide jobs to migrant workers who have returned to the state due to the COVID-19 pandemic. The scheme, called 'Mukhyamantri Shramik Yojana', aims to provide employment opportunities to migrant workers in various sectors, including construction, manufacturing, and agriculture. The government has announced that it will provide training and skill development programs to migrant workers to help them secure jobs in these sectors.

ticker: Power restoration in full swing in cyclone-affected West Bengal districts.
West Bengal government announces compensation for farmers affected by Cyclone Yaas.

stories.topic: Post Poll Violence in West Bengal

state_narrative.headline: Violence in West Bengal Post Election Results
state_narrative.summary: According to Zee News and Republic TV, there has been significant violence in West Bengal following the election results. The reports allege that the opposition parties are responsible for the majority of the violence and that the new government is taking swift action to control the situation.
claims_made: Opposition parties are primarily responsible for the violence.
The new government is taking swift action to control the situation.

verified_record.headline: Post Poll Violence in West Bengal: A Closer Look
verified_record.summary: According to The Wire and Scroll.in, the violence in West Bengal post-election is more nuanced. Reports indicate that violence is being perpetrated by both sides, and the new government's response has been slow and inadequate.
what_was_omitted: Violence is being perpetrated by both sides.
The new government's response has been slow and inadequate.
verified_record.data_check: According to reports from human rights organizations and independent journalists, there have been incidents of violence from multiple parties. Official data from the National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) shows a rise in politically motivated violence in the state.

gap_analysis.key_gap: The state-aligned media has not fully acknowledged the role of all parties in the violence.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The state-aligned media has downplayed the inadequacy of the new government's response.

state_narrative.headline: West Bengal's Economic Growth Under New Leadership
state_narrative.summary: According to ABP Ananda and News18 Bangla, West Bengal has seen unprecedented economic growth under the new leadership. The reports highlight significant infrastructure projects and an increase in investment.
claims_made: Unprecedented economic growth under new leadership.
Significant infrastructure projects and increase in investment.

verified_record.headline: Economic Development in West Bengal: A Critical Review
verified_record.summary: According to The Quint and India Spend, while there have been some infrastructure projects, the overall economic growth in West Bengal has been modest. Reports indicate that the state's economic indicators are still lagging behind national averages.
what_was_omitted: Overall economic growth has been modest.
State's economic indicators are lagging behind national averages.
verified_record.data_check: According to the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MOSPI), West Bengal's GDP growth rate is below the national average, and unemployment rates remain high.

gap_analysis.key_gap: The state-aligned media has overstated the economic growth and infrastructure development.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The state-aligned media has not provided a balanced view of the state's economic indicators.

state_narrative.headline: Healthcare Initiatives Transforming West Bengal
state_narrative.summary: According to Zee News and Republic TV, the new healthcare initiatives in West Bengal have significantly improved healthcare access and quality. The reports highlight the opening of new hospitals and the distribution of medical supplies.
claims_made: Significant improvement in healthcare access and quality.
Opening of new hospitals and distribution of medical supplies.

verified_record.headline: Healthcare in West Bengal: Reality Check
verified_record.summary: According to The Wire and FactChecker.in, while there have been some new hospitals and distribution of medical supplies, the overall healthcare system in West Bengal still faces significant challenges. Reports indicate shortages of medical staff and essential medicines.
what_was_omitted: Overall healthcare system still faces significant challenges.
Shortages of medical staff and essential medicines.
verified_record.data_check: According to the National Family Health Survey (NFHS) and the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, West Bengal's healthcare system still has significant gaps in terms of infrastructure, staffing, and essential medicines.

gap_analysis.key_gap: The state-aligned media has overstated the improvements in healthcare access and quality.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The state-aligned media has not adequately addressed the ongoing challenges in the healthcare system.

faq.question: How do I apply for Swasthya Sathi?
faq.answer:
**Swasthya Sathi** is West Bengal's universal health coverage scheme providing ₹5 lakh annual coverage per family.

**Eligibility:** All families in West Bengal with a valid ration card. The card is issued in the name of the woman head of household.

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your nearest **Duare Sarkar** camp (held periodically in every block/ward)
2. Or apply online at **swasthyasathi.gov.in**
3. Bring: Ration card, Aadhaar card, one passport photo
4. The smart card is issued on the spot at camps, or mailed within 15 days for online applications

**What's covered:** Hospitalization, surgeries, diagnostics, medicines at 1,500+ empanelled hospitals across Bengal.

**Helpline:** 1800-345-5384 (toll-free)

*(Source: Department of Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of West Bengal)*

faq.question: What is West Bengal's unemployment rate?
faq.answer:
According to the latest available data:

- **CMIE (April 2026):** West Bengal's unemployment rate is **6.8%**, marginally below the national average of 7.1%
- **PLFS 2024-25:** The state's labor force participation rate is 52.1% (male: 76.2%, female: 26.3%)
- **Youth unemployment (15-29 years):** Estimated at 18.4%, above the national average of 16.2%

**Key context:** West Bengal's female labor force participation rate (26.3%) is significantly below the national average (32.8%), indicating structural barriers to women's workforce entry.

The state's largest employers are agriculture (42%), services (36%), and manufacturing (22%). The informal sector accounts for approximately 89% of total employment.

*(Sources: CMIE Economic Outlook, Periodic Labour Force Survey 2024-25, MOSPI)*

faq.question: How do I file an RTI?
faq.answer:
**Right to Information (RTI)** allows any Indian citizen to request information from government bodies. Here's how:

**Step 1:** Write a simple application addressed to the **Public Information Officer (PIO)** of the relevant department.

**Step 2:** Include:
- Your name and address
- The specific information you're requesting (be precise)
- Write: "I request this information under the Right to Information Act, 2005"

**Step 3:** Pay ₹10 fee (₹0 for BPL card holders) via:
- Court fee stamp, postal order, or demand draft
- Online: **rtionline.gov.in** for central departments
- For WB state departments: Apply at the department office or via registered post

**Timeline:** PIO must respond within **30 days** (48 hours for life/liberty matters).

**If denied:** File a **First Appeal** to the appellate authority within 30 days. Then escalate to the **WB State Information Commission** at wbic.gov.in.

**Helpline:** WB Information Commission — 033-2214-5563

*(Source: RTI Act 2005, WB State Information Commission)*

faq.question: Explain the jute industry crisis
faq.answer:
West Bengal produces **75% of India's raw jute** and houses most of the country's jute mills, primarily along the Hooghly river belt (Howrah, Hooghly, North 24 Parganas).

**The crisis:**
- **Declining demand:** Synthetic alternatives have captured packaging markets that jute traditionally served
- **Aging infrastructure:** Most mills operate on machinery from the 1950s-70s, leading to low productivity
- **Wage disputes:** Workers earn ₹232/day — among the lowest industrial wages in India. Unions demand ₹350+
- **Mill closures:** 12 mills have closed permanently since 2019, displacing ~35,000 workers
- **Raw material price volatility:** Farmers face price crashes during peak harvest, discouraging cultivation

**Government response:**
- **Jute Packaging Material Act** mandates jute packaging for foodgrains and sugar — this keeps ~60% of mills running
- **Jute-ICARE scheme** (₹1,045 crore) for technology modernization
- **MSP for raw jute:** ₹5,350/quintal (2025-26)

**What workers can do:** Contact the Jute Workers' Helpline at the Labour Commissioner's office: 1800-345-4150.

*(Sources: Ministry of Textiles, IJMA Annual Report, The Wire, Indian Jute Mills Association)*

faq.question: What are my rights as a daily wage worker?
faq.answer:
As a daily wage or informal sector worker in West Bengal, you have these legal protections:

**Minimum Wage:**
- WB Minimum Wages Act sets floor wages by industry. Current rates range from ₹232-415/day depending on sector
- **Construction workers:** ₹356/day (skilled), ₹291/day (unskilled)
- Employers MUST pay at least minimum wage — violation is a criminal offense

**Key Rights:**
1. **Equal pay for equal work** — no gender-based wage discrimination (Article 39(d))
2. **MGNREGA:** 100 days guaranteed employment at ₹237/day. Apply at your Gram Panchayat.
3. **WB Building Workers' Welfare Board:** Register for pension (₹1,000/month), medical aid, children's education support
4. **ESI coverage** if your employer has 10+ workers and your salary is below ₹21,000/month
5. **No wage deduction** without written notice and consent

**If your wages are unpaid:**
1. File a complaint at the **District Labour Commissioner** office
2. Call the WB Labour Department helpline: **1800-345-4150**
3. You can also file a case at the **Labour Court** — no lawyer fees required

**Unions:** You have the constitutional right to form or join a trade union (Article 19(1)(c)).

*(Sources: WB Minimum Wages Act, MGNREGA Act 2005, Building & Other Construction Workers Act 1996)*

faq.question: Tell me about the MGNREGA scheme in WB
faq.answer:
**MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act)** guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household.

**In West Bengal:**
- **Wage rate:** ₹237/day (2025-26)
- **Person-days generated (2023-24):** 41.3 crore — West Bengal is among the top 5 states by utilization
- **Households benefited:** ~1.2 crore annually

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your **Gram Panchayat** office
2. Submit a written application demanding work (keep a receipt!)
3. Bring: Aadhaar card, bank passbook, passport photo
4. You'll receive a **Job Card** — this is your legal entitlement

**Your rights under MGNREGA:**
- Work must be provided **within 15 days** of application, or you're entitled to an unemployment allowance
- Wages must be paid **within 15 days** of work completion, directly to your bank account
- **Worksite facilities** are mandatory: drinking water, shade, first-aid, crèche for children
- **No contractor system** — work must be executed by the Gram Panchayat

**Common issues in WB:**
- Wage payment delays reported in several districts (average: 25-40 days vs mandated 15)
- Some panchayats show 'ghost beneficiaries' — report this via the Grievance Portal

**Grievance redressal:** nrega.nic.in/netnrega → 'Register Grievance' or call 1800-345-22-44

*(Sources: mgnregs.nic.in, Ministry of Rural Development, CAG Performance Audit 2024-25)*
